decisions.decision: Dictionary pull merge matches by Dictionary.code (DB-unique), not id, exclusively inside sync_client.py's _apply_pull_page — app/services/merge.py (server-side shared logic) left untouched
decisions.rationale: Local and server Dictionary tables were independently imported by scripts/import_master_pricelist.py, generating different UUIDs for the same product code on each side; Dictionary is push-exempt (client never sends it upstream) so this is a client-pull-only fix, not a shared-merge-core change

decisions.decision: Server-role detection uses settings.database_url.startswith('postgresql') rather than checking sync_server_url
decisions.rationale: sync_server_url being blank is ALSO the normal state for a never-yet-paired local client (per SRV-03 offline-first design), so it can't distinguish 'this IS the server' from 'this is an unpaired client'; database_url dialect is the one architecturally-guaranteed signal (SRV-01/02: only the deployed central server runs PostgreSQL)

decisions.decision: reset_business_data.py wipes ONLY products/batches/customers/customer_contacts/sales/operations/cash_movements; warehouses/users/device_tokens/dictionary/catalog_prices/active_catalog/sync_state are explicitly out of scope
decisions.rationale: User's explicit choice via AskUserQuestion mid-session ('Удалить всё бизнес-данные (Recommended)' over the 'including warehouses' alternative); CLI-only with mandatory typed confirmation ('УДАЛИТЬ'), no --force/--yes flag anywhere — user's explicit choice ('Только скрипт (CLI), вручную') over a /settings button

decisions.decision: load_test_data.py calls register_transfer exactly 5 times (not 10) to land on exactly 10 transfer-type Operation rows
decisions.rationale: register_transfer writes TWO transfer ops per call (source -qty, dest +qty, confirmed in app/services/transfers.py) — calling it 10 times would produce 20 rows, breaking the '10 operations of each type' requirement
